panama.profile:
Here is a detailed breakdown of Panama's confirmed squad for the **2026 FIFA World Cup**, based on the latest available information:

---

🇵🇦 **PANAMA — 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **🧑‍💼 Manager: Thomas Christiansen**
Thomas Christiansen named Panama's 26-man squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup on May 26. He has previously managed AEK Larnaca and APOEL Nicosia in Cyprus, Leeds United, and Union Saint-Gilloise in Belgium, and with Panama he has set the national record for most international matches managed.

- **🥅 Goalkeepers**
The confirmed goalkeepers are Orlando Mosquera (Al-Fayha FC, Saudi Pro League), Luis Mejía (Club Nacional, Uruguayan League), and César Samudio (CD Marathón, Honduran League).

- **🛡️ Key Defenders & Midfielders**
Players such as Amir Murillo, Aníbal Godoy, Fidel Escobar, Yoel Bárcenas, and Orlando Mosquera will once again lead the national team. Captain Aníbal Godoy holds 157 caps — a national record — and plays for San Diego FC.

- **⚽ Key Attackers

uzbekistan.profile:
Here is a detailed breakdown of Uzbekistan's confirmed squad for the **2026 FIFA World Cup**:

---

🐺 **UZBEKISTAN — 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **🧑‍💼 Manager — Fabio Cannavaro:**
Fabio Cannavaro will lead Uzbekistan at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The legendary Italian defender and 2006 World Cup champion took over the project with the mission of bringing experience, competitive mentality, and tactical discipline to a debuting national team. He took over the "White Wolves" bench in October 2025, replacing Timur Kapadze, the local coach who led Uzbekistan to its historic qualification.

- **🧤 Goalkeepers:**
The goalkeeping department features O'tkir Yusupov, Botirali Ergashev, and Abduvohid Ne'matov. Yusupov (Navbahor) is the expected No. 1, with this position boasting both experience and healthy competition, as a goalkeeper's composure, reflexes, and communication with the defense are vital at a World Cup.

- **🛡️ Defenders — Led by Abdukodir Khusanov:**
The standout player in the Uzbekistan squad is Abdukodir

colombia.profile:
Here is a confirmed breakdown of Colombia's 2026 FIFA World Cup squad:

---

🇨🇴 **COLOMBIA – 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **Manager:** Coach **Néstor Lorenzo** announced the 26-player squad on Monday, May 25. Under the technical direction of the Argentine, the national team faces its **seventh World Cup appearance**.

- **Goalkeepers:** Experience is the main focus in Colombia's goalkeeping group — all three GKs are over 30. That includes **37-year-old David Ospina**, who is available for what could be his final major tournament. His presence brings important leadership, but **Álvaro Montero** is expected to be the first-choice starter. **Camilo Vargas** rounds out the group but is likely to play a supporting role.

- **Defenders:** Fans will recognise plenty of household names in Colombia's defensive ranks. **Dávinson Sánchez** (Galatasaray) has been a stalwart in their backline. **Yerry Mina** (Cagliari) brings physicality, and **Daniel Muñoz** (Crystal Palace) has been a standout, having helped his club win the FA Cup in 2025.

- **Midfielders:**

curaçao.profile:
Here is what is confirmed about Curaçao's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, based on available sources:

---

🇨🇼 **Curaçao — 2026 FIFA World Cup Squad Overview**

- **Historic Debut:** This is Curaçao's debut at a FIFA World Cup, marking their historic first qualification — achieved on their fourth attempt. They make history as the smallest nation ever to qualify for the men's World Cup tournament.

- **Manager:** Curaçao is led by Dutch manager **Dick Advocaat**, who guided the nation to one of the three direct CONCACAF berths alongside Panama and Haiti. However, Advocaat led the squad through qualification but stepped down due to his daughter's health.

- **Squad Size & Announcement:** Curaçao announced a full **26-player squad** for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The final squad was announced on **May 18**.

- **GK & Most-Capped Player:** Team captain **Leandro Bacuna** anchors the squad, and veteran goalkeeper **Eloy Room** are the country's most-capped players, with **71 caps each**.

- **Key Players by Position:**

germany.profile:
Here is a confirmed, factual breakdown of Germany's 2026 FIFA World Cup squad:

---

🇩🇪 **Germany – 2026 FIFA World Cup Squad Overview**

- **Manager:** Julian Nagelsmann leads the side, who eased through their World Cup qualification group, recording five wins from six matches. Nagelsmann has opted for a mixture of experienced players and young talent.

- **Goalkeeper:** The 40-year-old Manuel Neuer was a surprise inclusion in Nagelsmann's 26-man squad. Neuer had retired from international play after Euro 2024, but is now set to make his fifth World Cup appearance, with Nagelsmann confirming he is the first choice ahead of Oliver Baumann and Alexander Nübel.

- **Key Defenders:** Antonio Rüdiger returns following a prolonged injury, and David Raum from RB Leipzig is also part of the squad. Captain Joshua Kimmich, with over 100 caps, is equally capable in midfield or at right-back.

- **Key Midfielders:** Joshua Kimmich was named team captain. Kai Havertz and Felix Nmecha also return following prolonged periods of injury.

brazil.profile:
Here is a detailed breakdown of Brazil's confirmed squad for the **2026 FIFA World Cup**:

---

🇧🇷 **BRAZIL – 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **👨‍💼 Manager:** Carlo Ancelotti has officially announced Brazil's final 26-man squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The Italian success coach was appointed to the job two months after the dismissal of Dorival Júnior. Ancelotti is the most successful manager in Champions League history and brings a reputation for getting the best out of elite attacking talent.

- **🧤 Goalkeeper:** Liverpool's Alisson is the front-runner to start between the sticks, though the Seleção also have the option of former Manchester City goalkeeper Ederson, who has bags of experience and numerous title wins from his time under Pep Guardiola.

- **🛡️ Defenders:** The defence is experienced and well marshalled by the indefatigable Marquinhos, playing in front of the still world-class Alisson. PSG centre-back Marquinhos remains the cornerstone of their defence, while Gabriel (Arsenal) has been ever-present at the back and offers a goalscoring threat at set-piece situations.

haiti.profile:
Here is what can be confirmed from the available search results about Haiti's 2026 FIFA World Cup squad:

---

🇭🇹 **Haiti – 2026 FIFA World Cup Squad Overview**

- **Manager: Sébastien Migné** — The 53-year-old French coach played professionally between 1989 and 2002, then began his coaching career in the 2010s mainly as an assistant and youth-team coach before taking on senior national team roles across Africa and, later, Haiti. He successfully qualified Haiti for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

- **Historic Return:** Haiti will make only their second appearance at the FIFA World Cup — and their first in 52 years. Les Grenadiers lost all three games on their tournament debut in 1974. After topping their 2026 qualifying group, the Caribbean nation will hope to pick up their first World Cup point in a group containing record five-time champions Brazil, Scotland, and Morocco.

- **GK – Johny Placide (Captain/Lead GK):** Haiti's final 26-player roster is led by veteran goalkeeper Johny Placide. The squad includes three goalkeepers, eight defenders, seven midfielders, and eight forwards.

- **MID – Jean-Ricner Bellegarde:** A Premier League star

cape verde.profile:
Here is a confirmed, detailed breakdown of Cape Verde's squad for the **2026 FIFA World Cup**:

---

🇨🇻 **CAPE VERDE — 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **Historic Debut & Manager:** Cape Verde officially confirmed the 26 players who will represent the country at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where the Blue Sharks will compete in football's biggest tournament for the very first time. The team is managed by Pedro Brito, better known as **Bubista**, and will compete in **Group H** alongside Spain, Uruguay, and Saudi Arabia. Bubista has taken Cape Verde to three major tournaments since taking over in 2020; in 2023 they reached the AFCON quarter-finals, and he holds an impressive 48% win rate as manager.

- **GK — Vozinha (vice-captain):** Goalkeeper Vozinha is the oldest player in the squad at 39 years old and serves as vice-captain. Led by Vozinha, the Blue Sharks are difficult to break down and remain disciplined without possession.

- **DEF — Logan Costa & backline:** Villarreal's **Logan Costa** is the lone representative from Europe's top five leagues and anchors the defence.

mexico.profile:
Here is a comprehensive breakdown of Mexico's confirmed 2026 FIFA World Cup squad:

---

🇲🇽 **Mexico — 2026 FIFA World Cup Squad**

- **Manager:** Head coach **Javier Aguirre** officially announced his 26-man Mexico World Cup 2026 squad on Sunday, June 1, 2026. Twelve players in the roster endured a heartbreaking group stage exit in Qatar 2022, and these battle-tested veterans add experience alongside a crop of young and exciting talents.

- **Goalkeeper:** World Cup cult hero **Guillermo Ochoa** will join Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi as the only players in history to appear in six different World Cups. Ochoa — who will turn 41 during the tournament — is expected to feature in his record-equalling sixth FIFA World Cup.

- **Key Defenders:** Most of the uncertainty surrounding the squad centered around the defense. The backline features experienced players with World Cup pedigree, including **Edson Álvarez**, who is also a key figure across the midfield and defensive line. The squad features several players with previous World Cup experience, including Guillermo Ochoa, **Edson Álvarez**, Raúl Jiménez, Luis Romo, and Orbelín Pineda.

switzerland.profile:
Here is a detailed breakdown of Switzerland's confirmed 2026 FIFA World Cup squad:

---

🇨🇭 **SWITZERLAND – 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP SQUAD**

- **Manager – Murat Yakin:** Yakin, 51, has led Switzerland since August 2021. A Swiss-born coach and former defender, he was capped 49 times for his country and won five Swiss titles as a player. He guided Switzerland to the Round of 16 at Qatar 2022, then to the Euro 2024 quarterfinals, where they beat holders Italy before losing to England on penalties.

- **Goalkeepers:** The squad is led between the posts by **Gregor Kobel** (Borussia Dortmund), with Yvon Mvogo (Lorient) and Marvin Keller (Young Boys) as backups. Kobel has built a fine reputation at Dortmund and will be among the most reputable goalkeepers at the World Cup, having been left with big gloves to fill following Yann Sommer's international exit.
